subject: Databases
title: BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK
description: A 60-second lesson on BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK.

hook.text: BEGIN; UPDATE accounts SET balance=balance-100 WHERE id=1; UPDATE accounts SET balance=balance+100 WHERE id=2; COMMIT;

buildup.text: BEGIN starts a transaction. All statements after it are tentative until COMMIT makes them permanent.

discovery.text: ROLLBACK undoes everything since BEGIN. It's the escape hatch when something goes wrong.

twist.text: In autocommit mode (the default for most clients), every single statement is its own transaction.

climax.text: Explicit transactions let you group statements. Autocommit is fine for one-liners but dangerous for multi-step work.

punchline.text: BEGIN groups work. COMMIT saves it. ROLLBACK undoes it.

quiz.question: What does autocommit mode mean?
options: Each individual SQL statement is automatically wrapped in its own transaction
The database never commits any changes
All changes are committed only when the connection closes
